UseUpstream._comment_payments: JEB-1484 / GR3 — route the cash-settlement ledger post THROUGH UPG. DEFAULT-OFF: UPG's JEB-1484 PR is owner-gated and NOT auto-deployed, so this stays false until the UPG PR is approved + deployed. Flip via FeatureFlags__UseUpstream__Payments=true thereafter.

FeatureFlags._comment_kyc: JEBV4-8 — MSI dev/staging AUTO-APPROVE. When ON a first-time KYC submission is adjudicated Verified + the jeeber role granted inline (kyc-service /review seam + user-management available_roles append; ADR-0004 gateway-composed grant) with NO admin step, and the submit response returns state=Verified so the app renders 'approved' and the JeeberRoleActivator fires with NO re-login. Kills the JEBV4-8 admin blocker. Set FeatureFlags__Kyc__AutoApprove=false to restore manual admin review.

Heartbeat._comment: S06 / ADR-HB-001 — presence cutover to the NEW reusable heart-beat service. DEFAULT-OFF this round: heart-beat is pending the one-time GHA deploy of the swarm service (Services:HeartBeat:BaseUrl points at the confirmed internal port http://192.168.2.50:10075/, but FeatureFlags:Heartbeat:Enabled is false so the gateway never dials it). While off the availability surface keeps using the live delivery-service presence wire (zero behaviour change; the delivery path is the instant rollback target). Flip via FeatureFlags__Heartbeat__Enabled=true (a deploy workflow_dispatch input), staging-first, ONLY after heart-beat is live, its /health/ready + a real PATCH /v1/presence round-trip smoke-pass, AND the FeatureFlags__Heartbeat__ServiceAuthKey secret is injected. roleKey is the OPAQUE consumer namespace heart-beat stores but never interprets.
Heartbeat._comment_serviceAuthKey: S06 AUTH CONTRACT — ServiceAuthKey is the static X-Service-Auth-Key the gateway presents to heart-beat (the path heart-beat's auth middleware constant-time compares). SECRET — committed BLANK; inject the real value via FeatureFlags__Heartbeat__ServiceAuthKey from a swarm secret, identical to the HEARTBEAT_SERVICE_AUTH_KEY the heart-beat repo holds. Do NOT flip Enabled=true in any environment where this is blank (heart-beat would 401).

Features._comment: DevEndpoints MUST stay false in production (charter N0-2). The additive /dev/* test-harness routes (DevController) return 404 while off, so the prod surface is indistinguishable from no-such-endpoint. The gateway never auto-seeds. Do NOT commit true here.
Features._comment_swagger: Swagger MUST stay false in production. /swagger* returns 404 while off, indistinguishable from no-such-endpoint. jeeb.fds-1.com is PUBLIC: enabling Swagger leaks the full route surface + schemas, so it is admin-role-gated (non-admin => 404) and toggled ONLY via the deploy-to-jeeb.yml swagger_ui input (env-add Features__Swagger__Enabled=true). NEVER flip ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT to enable it (that opens the unauthenticated Dev/Testing branch). Do NOT commit true here.

Auth._comment: Auth:Otp configures the production phone sign-in OTP orchestration (AuthOtpController, /v1/auth/otp/*). ApplicationId is the Jeeb tenant's application id in the shared one-time-password service, forwarded on every SendOTP/ValidateOTP. The real id is injected at deploy time via env var Auth__Otp__ApplicationId (per fleet law: live config via GHA, not committed secrets); the committed placeholder is intentionally empty. ttlSeconds is the contract constant surfaced on request.

JeebStateService._comment: jeeb-state-service (olivium-dev/jeeb-state-service, .NET 8, Jeeb-owned) — ADR-001-rev2 durable state behind the stateless gateway. Host port 10073, prod-IP convention 192.168.2.50. Backs R1 idempotency + R8 locks/rate-limit (bounce-survivable) and durable write-through for refresh families/KYC/ratings/disputes/strikes/OTP-escalation.

Cdn._comment: cdn-service deployed on the Jeeb swarm at host port 10072. Health route: GET /health/ready. Serves JEB-527/JEB-519/JEB-59. Flip FeatureFlags:UseUpstream:Cdn to true to route through the real upstream.

Compliment._comment: compliment-service deployed on the Jeeb swarm at host port 10036 [PG read]. Consumed read-only by the DB-probe proxy GET /api/compliments/list?userId=.. -> upstream GET /list.

Realtime._comment: realtime-comunication-service (olivium-dev/realtime-comunication-service, Elixir/Phoenix 'LiveComm') deployed on the Jeeb swarm at host port 10069 (prod-IP convention: 192.168.2.50). Health route: GET /health. Serves JEB-1453/1449/1432/626/444/50/51/52.

ContractSigning._comment: contract-signing-service (FastAPI) deployed on the Jeeb swarm at host port 10071. Health route: GET /health. Flip FeatureFlags:UseUpstream:ContractSigning to true to route through the real upstream.

Kyc._comment: kyc-service (S03/ADR-0004) LIVE on the Jeeb swarm at host port 10074 (own jeeb_kyc Postgres, ARCH-clean — no sibling calls). Health GET /health. FeatureFlags:UseUpstream:Kyc is ON; the KycBffSeam routes 100% to this owning service and the gateway holds zero KYC state.

HeartBeat._comment: heart-beat (S06 / ADR-HB-001) — NEW reusable presence service (Go + Redis), owns the online bit + lastSeenAt recency + TTL idle-sweep. Internal swarm port 10075 (confirmed free against the live fleet port map; the heart-beat swarm service binds host 192.168.2.50:10075). FeatureFlags:Heartbeat:Enabled is DEFAULT-OFF everywhere, so the gateway never dials this host until the flag flips. Flip ONLY after the heart-beat swarm service is live, its /health/ready is 200, the FeatureFlags__Heartbeat__ServiceAuthKey secret is injected (== heart-beat's HEARTBEAT_SERVICE_AUTH_KEY), and a real PATCH /v1/presence round-trip smoke-passes.

ServiceContractSigningApi._comment: Sibling-gateway Service<Svc>Api naming alias for Services:ContractSigning. contract-signing-service deployed at host port 10071.
